title: Adjunction of roots to nilpotent groups
journal: Proceedings of the Glasgow Mathematical Association
abstract: For any nilpotent groupBof class c and any given elementhofBgenerating the subgroupH, Wiegold [1] has shown that if, in addition, [B, H] has exponentprfor some primepand integerr, thenBcan be embedded in a nilpotent groupGsuch thatGalso containspsth root forh(s ≧ 1). In fact, Wiegold has gone further and calculated an upper bound for the class ofGin terms of the variablesc, p, r, s.
